response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer, but he does not meet the role's mandatory requirements. His professional C++ experience amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2007-2008 plus hobby and thesis work, far short of 5-10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development. His career since has centered on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. He has no fixed income, capital markets, or investment banking systems experience (RIDE Capital was a Node/TypeScript fintech product role), and his database background is MySQL/MongoDB rather than SQL Server or Oracle. The gap between his profile and the core stack and domain is too wide to justify an interview for this position.
strengths: Deep, broad engineering experience (20+ years) across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Strong software craftsmanship practices: TDD, CI/CD, test-framework authorship, and technical teaching
Real low-latency systems experience handling millions of requests per second at Sociomantic
concerns: Professional C++ experience is minimal (~7 months) and nearly two decades old, versus the required 5-10 years on Windows
No fixed income, capital markets, or investment banking domain experience
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; SQL background is MySQL/MongoDB

key_factors.factor: No capital markets / fixed income domain background
